zh.Leicester, Leicestershire, England: 英國萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont於1480年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特時，她的父親Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan20歲，母親Lady Isabel Vermandois21歲。她於1505年與Gilbert De Clare在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1505年之後在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.Leicester, Leicestershire, England: 英国萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont于1480年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特时，她的父亲Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan20岁，母亲Lady Isabel Vermandois21岁。她于1505年与Gilbert De Clare在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1505年之后在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont was born in 1480, in Leicester, Leicestershire, England, her father, Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan, was 20 and her mother, Lady Isabel Vermandois, was 21. She married Gilbert De Clare in 1505, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died after 1505, in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont est née en 1480, à Leicester, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Lady Isabel Vermandois, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Gilbert De Clare en 1505, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée après 1505, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont 1480 in Leicester, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan 20 und ihre Mutter Lady Isabel Vermandois 21. Sie hat 1505 in England Gilbert De Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb nach 1505 in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont è nata il nel 1480 nel Leicester, Leicestershire, England, sua padre, Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Lady Isabel Vermandois, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Gilbert De Clare nel 1505, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì dopo il 1505, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Leicester, Leicestershire, England: イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアレアイセスター
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumontが1480年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアレアイセスターで生まれた時，父Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelanは20歳，母Lady Isabel Vermandoisは21歳であった。1505年にイギリスでGilbert De Clareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1505年以後にイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont(이)가 1480년 영국 레이스스터실르 레이스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Isabel Vermandois(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1505년 영국에서 Gilbert De Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1505년 이후 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont nasceu em 1480, em Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Lady Isabel Vermandois, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Gilbert De Clare em 1505, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu após 1505, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont родилась в 1480 г. в местности лисестир, лисестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Isabel Vermandois, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gilbert De Clare в 1505 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла после 1505 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont nació en 1480, en Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Earl Robert Corbet de Beaumont-le-Roger, Earl of Licester,Comte Muelan, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Lady Isabel Vermandois, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Gilbert De Clare en 1505, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió después de 1505, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
